changes_summary: Revised plan to address all 5 significant flags: (1) moved pendingOpsRef increment/decrement for resize to TimelineCanvas where the full session lifecycle is managed, preventing leak on cancel; (2) changed external drop protection to per-upload tracking inside each fire-and-forget IIFE; (3) dropped the undo/redo race fix — confirmed skipHistory:true on save-success means onBeforeCommit is never called, so no race exists; (4) corrected the event bus to target useTimelineCommit (not useTimelineSave) and added scheduleSaveRef as the 4th ref to replace; (5) replaced gradual compat-shim migration with explicit consumer migration step — all 3 hot consumers plus 4 light consumers are migrated to sub-context hooks in the same step.
questions: For the context split in Step 14: the plan proposes 2 sub-contexts (Data + Ops). Data holds ~30 read-only props + refs; Ops holds ~35 callback props. An alternative is 3 sub-contexts (Data, Selection, Ops) which gives finer-grained isolation for components that only read selection state. Worth the extra context?

The brief mentions useAssetManagement concurrent asset operations — should we make handleAssetDrop's track-creation-if-needed fully atomic via a combined applyEdit payload (cleaner, but requires applyEdit to handle track+row+meta in one call), or use a synchronous dataRef guard (simpler, relies on JS single-threaded execution between awaits)?

success_criteria.criterion: All 71 existing tests pass after every phase
Resize pendingOpsRef: increments on handleResizePointerDown, decrements on BOTH normal endResize and cancelled endResize (new test)
External drop pendingOpsRef: per-upload increment inside for-loop, per-upload decrement in each async IIFE's finally block (new test)
No concurrent track creation race in multi-file drops (handleAssetDrop uses atomic applyEdit or synchronous dataRef guard)
SortableRow and ClipAction wrapped in React.memo to prevent cascade re-renders during drag/resize
All 4 ref-wiring patterns (onSaveSuccessRef, onBeforeCommitRef, pruneSelectionRef, scheduleSaveRef) replaced with typed event bus
TimelineEditorContextValue split into Data + Ops sub-contexts — no single context with 80+ properties
All 7 known consumers (TimelineEditor, PropertiesPanel, PreviewPanel, VideoEditorShell, AssetPanel, CompactPreview, useSelectedMediaClips) migrated to sub-context hooks
UseTimelineDataResult adapter type eliminated — downstream hooks accept specific args
Selection logic extracted from useTimelineState into dedicated useTimelineSelection hook
SortableRow receives per-clip resize override (not whole map) to enable effective memo
Each sub-context has a convenience hook (useTimelineEditorData, useTimelineEditorOps)
Undo/redo during save audited and confirmed safe (skipHistory:true prevents onBeforeCommit during save reconciliation)
useDerivedTimeline audited and confirmed clean (no changes needed)
Manual smoke tests pass (drag, resize, drop, cancel-resize, undo/redo, preview playback)

assumptions: applyEdit is synchronous in its state update (updates dataRef.current immediately) — confirmed by reading useTimelineCommit.ts:152 which does `dataRef.current = nextData` synchronously.
The 4 refs to replace with event bus are: onSaveSuccessRef, onBeforeCommitRef, pruneSelectionRef, scheduleSaveRef — all confirmed in current code.
useDerivedTimeline's configSignatureRef/stableResolvedConfigRef pattern was already removed in the previous refactor — the current hook is clean (90 lines, pure useMemo).

2-way context split (Data + Ops) is the target granularity. Data changes frequently; Ops (memoized callbacks) changes rarely. This delivers the main render isolation benefit without excessive context nesting.
The deprecated useTimelineEditorContext() compat hook exists for any consumers not migrated in Step 15, but the 7 known consumers are all migrated explicitly.
structure_warnings: Each step section should reference at least one file in backticks.
